Fix Function App startup failure: switch to Oryx remote build for Python dependencies (#8)

* Initial plan

* fix: switch to Oryx remote build to resolve Python binary extension version mismatch

The deploy workflow was pre-installing packages on the CI runner (Python 3.11)
and deploying them with scm-do-build-during-deployment=false. When the Function
App runtime uses a different Python version (e.g. 3.13), binary C extensions
like _cffi_backend (from cffi/cryptography used by azure-identity) fail to load.

Changes:
- deploy-function.yml: install deps locally for tests only (no --target);
  set scm-do-build-during-deployment=true so Azure Oryx builds packages on
  the actual runtime environment
- function-app.bicep: add SCM_DO_BUILD_DURING_DEPLOYMENT=1 and
  ENABLE_ORYX_BUILD=true app settings to ensure the Oryx pipeline runs

commit.message:
v0.3.9: fix Wi-Fi hotspot not appearing on first boot

Three root causes found by inspecting v0.3.8 image via Parallels + loop-mount:

1. wpa_supplicant was fighting hostapd for wlan0
   - Bookworm Lite enables wpa_supplicant by default (Wi-Fi client mode)
   - Removed multi-user.target.wants/wpa_supplicant.service symlink directly
     in chroot (systemctl disable is unreliable without running D-Bus)

2. userconf.txt was written with no password hash (interactive login prompt)
   - openssl passwd -6 -stdin outputs nothing on Linux — not portable
   - Fixed: openssl passwd -6 'logfalcon' (direct arg, works everywhere)
   - Fixed: userconfig.service symlink removed directly (was silently failing
     via 'systemctl disable' in chroot)

3. dnsmasq starts before wlan0 is configured (bind-interfaces race)
   - Added systemd overrides for dnsmasq and hostapd:
     After=sys-subsystem-net-devices-wlan0.device, Restart=on-failure

Also adds scripts/inspect-image.sh: mounts any .img/.img.xz via Parallels
or Docker and audits all hotspot configs, services, binary, and known issues.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: toml 1.0 compat + crate_deps pipeline tests; fix approx PI lint

Merges two bug fixes from dev:

1. fix(tests): replace approximate PI literal with std::f64::consts::PI
   Clippy's approx_constant lint (-D warnings) rejected 3.14 in
   verification_serde_test.rs, failing all three CI jobs.

2. fix(emit): use toml::from_str instead of .parse() for toml 1.0 compat
   toml 1.0 removed str.parse::<toml::Value>() for full TOML documents.
   all_crate_deps() silently returned empty, so every #[elicit_tool]
   was missing all dependencies, breaking all 32 elicit_server smoke tests.

Testing gap closed:
   - elicit_url/tests/emit_deps_test.rs: 3-stage dep pipeline tests
   - elicit_server/tests/emit_smoke_test.rs: 5 crate_deps pipeline tests

CI: all 3 platforms green (run 22890919752)

keyboard focus management. Implements automatic Tab/Shift+Tab navigation
through focusable widgets (Button, TextInput).

- Add SetFocused/IsFocused to PublicWidget interface and BasePublicWidget
- Add Focusable interface with CanTakeFocus method

- Remove duplicate focused field from TextInput (use BasePublicWidget's)
- Add comprehensive tests: TestFocusNavigation, TestFocusableInterface,
  TestKeyboardActivation, TestFocusChainAutoBuilding
- Update doc.go with focus management examples

Validation:
- GOOS=windows go build ./... passes
- GOOS=windows go vet ./... passes

commit.message:
feat: VM snapshot/restore for fast cold start (#8)

* feat: VM snapshot/restore with SnapshotManager and integration tests

Add snapshot/restore support using Cloud Hypervisor's native API for
fast VM cold start (~60ms restore vs ~460ms boot).

Core additions:
- SnapshotManager (snapshot.rs): golden snapshot lifecycle
  - ensure_golden_snapshot(): boot minimal VM, verify agent, snapshot
  - restore_vm(): per-instance config.json path rewriting + symlinked
    memory file (avoids copying 128MB per restore)
  - Lazy creation on first use, reused across all restores

- VmManager extensions (vm.rs):
  - create_and_boot_vm_for_snapshot(): boots without virtiofs to avoid
    vhost-user protocol state issue that blocks restore
  - from_restored(): wraps a restored VM into VmManager
  - add_net()/add_net_to_socket(): hot-add virtio-net post-restore
  - ch_pid(): accessor for process management
  - Cleaned up restore()/receive_migration() to use api_request_to_socket
    instead of raw HTTP with inconsistent response parsing

Integration tests (3 new):
- test_snapshot_and_restore: basic snapshot/restore cycle
- test_snapshot_manager_golden_lifecycle: golden create + 2 concurrent restores
- test_snapshot_restore_with_networking: restore + net hot-add

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: integrate snapshot restore into VM pool warming

Wire SnapshotManager into VmPool so pool warming uses snapshot restore
(~60ms) instead of cold boot (~460ms) when a golden snapshot exists.
Falls back to full boot transparently if no snapshot is available.

- VmPool now owns a SnapshotManager
- warm() creates golden snapshot lazily on first use
- create_warm_vm() tries snapshot restore, falls back to cold boot
- snapshot_manager() exposes the manager for external use

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add support for postgres schema selection (#475)

* feat: add support for postgres schema selection

Add support for selecting a PostgreSQL schema instead of always using
'public'. The schema is extracted from the connection URL's options
parameter (search_path), following PostgreSQL's native libpq format.

Changes:
- Add _parse_schema_from_url() to extract schema from connection URL
- Thread schema parameter through all extraction methods with 'public' default
- Add pg_namespace JOINs for correct cross-schema disambiguation
- Add schema input field in DatabaseModal (PostgreSQL only)
- Add comprehensive unit tests for URL schema parsing
- Update documentation with custom schema configuration guide

Based on PR #373 by sirudog with the following fixes:
- Fix pg_namespace JOIN order in extract_columns_info to prevent
  duplicate rows when same-named tables exist across schemas
- Fix regex to require '=' separator (prevents mis-capture edge cases)
- Improve $user handling to loop through all schemas instead of only
  checking first two positions
- Fix pylint line-too-long in test file

Co-authored-by: sirudog <1550561+sirudog@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: make parse_schema_from_url public to fix CI pylint

Rename _parse_schema_from_url to parse_schema_from_url since the
method is already documented for external use and tested directly.
This eliminates W0212 (protected-access) warnings that cause CI
pylint to fail with exit code 4.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review comments on PR #475

- Add constraint_schema qualifier to key_column_usage JOINs in
  extract_columns_info to prevent cross-schema constraint name
  collisions
- Sanitize schema input in DatabaseModal to strip non-identifier
  characters before building the URL options
- Add edge case tests: empty tokens, blank quoted tokens, repeated
  $user entries

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: remove accidentally committed build artifacts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address copilot reviewer comments on PR #475

- Fix regex to capture search_path values with spaces after commas
  (e.g. $user, public) by matching up to next -c option or EOL
- Set session search_path explicitly after connecting so sample
  queries resolve to the correct schema
- Use versionless PostgreSQL docs link (/docs/current/)
- Clarify case-sensitivity note for schema names in troubleshooting

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: gitignore build artifacts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace ReDoS-vulnerable regex in parse_schema_from_url

Replace (.+?)(?=\s+-c|\s*$) with [^\s,]+(?:\s*,\s*[^\s,]+)* to
eliminate polynomial backtracking flagged by CodeQL. The new pattern
uses unambiguous character classes with no overlapping quantifiers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: validate schema input instead of silent sanitization, fix doc URL encoding

- DatabaseModal: Show validation error for invalid schema characters instead
  of silently stripping them. Throw error on submit if invalid chars present.
- docs: URL-encode the example URL to prevent copy/paste connection failures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: revert doc URL to readable form to fix spellcheck

The URL-encoded form (-csearch_path%3Dmy_schema) inside the Liquid
capture block triggers spellcheck failures ('csearch', 'Dmy'). Reverted
to readable form since Python's urlparse handles both formats fine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add missing tech terms to spellcheck wordlist

Add terms from AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md (added in staging merge) to the
spellcheck wordlist: config, docstring, dotenv, ESLint, HSTS, init,
Middleware, monorepo, PRs, pylint, pytest, Radix, Zod, and error class
names. Also fix DockerHub capitalization.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: ensure DB connection cleanup on error and add cursor type hints

- Wrap psycopg2 connection/cursor in try/finally so they are always
  closed, even when extract_tables_info or extract_relationships raises
- Set conn/cursor to None after explicit close to avoid double-close
  in the finally block
- Add Any type hints to cursor parameters on extract_tables_info,
  extract_columns_info, extract_foreign_keys, extract_relationships,
  and _execute_sample_query

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: increase timeout for multi-step E2E chat tests

Mark three tests that perform multiple LLM round-trips with
test.slow() to triple their timeout (60s → 180s), preventing
spurious CI failures when LLM responses are slow:

- multiple sequential queries maintain conversation history
- switching databases clears chat history
- duplicate record shows user-friendly error message
